Title:
Title-Source: body_internal|generated
No-Joke-Found: false

When I was 11 years old, in 1938, I stood next to my Grandfather at a 
urinal in a men's restroom in Pecos, Texas.

Now mind you, my Grandfather was a retired Baptist preacher and my 
perception of him was that he would never tell me anything that could 
not repeated to others.  So when I heard him make the following 
statement, I have told it hundreds of times over the last 60 plus 
years.

As we stood at the urinal, doing what men and boys do at urinals, my 
Grandfather let out a loud sigh and said, "A corn is a corn, and a 
bean is a bean.  But, brother, a pea is a relief."
